IEEPA Reciprocal Tariff baseline of 10% on all commercial imports into the US, effective April 5, 2025. Stacks on top of MFN duty. Chapter exclusions (Section 232, pharma, semis, gold, energy, lumber) are separate -10% rows under the same program.
IEEPA Fentanyl Tariff: +20% additional duty on goods of PRC / Hong Kong origin entering the US, effective Feb 4, 2025. Stacks on top of MFN and any other Section 301 / IEEPA programs.

IEEPA Mexico Border Tariff: +25% on Mexico-origin goods NOT qualifying under USMCA, effective March 4, 2025. USMCA-qualifying goods are exempt — engine treats all MX-origin as subject unless preferentialApplied.
IEEPA Canada Border Tariff: +25% on Canada-origin goods NOT qualifying under USMCA, effective March 4, 2025. Energy products from CA carry a reduced 10% rate.
Post-Geneva-deal effective reciprocal rate on China-origin goods: 30% total (10% baseline + 20% additional). This row contributes the +20% on top of the universal baseline.

Section 301 statutory-review modification — PV cells/modules raised 25% → 50% effective Sep 27 2024. Bifacial cells for utility-scale projects retain partial exclusion (verify per project).
